pi_items.source: Web search results citing meteocast.net and other astronomical sources. Multiple sources confirm December 2024 Tucson sunsets occur around 18:29-18:52, not 17:15-17:30
Web search results showing December 13, 2024 sunset at 18:51. Meteocast.net data cited in search results: December 13 sunset 18:51, December 15 sunset 18:52
Astronomical twilight (end of civil twilight) typically occurs 30-60 minutes after sunset. With sunset at ~18:51, astronomical twilight end would be ~19:45-20:00
Standard astronomical calculation: astronomical twilight ends when sun is 18 degrees below horizon. For mid-December at Tucson latitude (32.2°N), this occurs ~55 minutes after sunset
MoonGiant.com WebFetch: Moon illumination on December 13, 2024 was 96%. Full moon was December 15, 2024
Multiple sources (MoonGiant, moongiant.com phase data) indicate moon illumination on December 13, 2024 ranged from 96-98%
Unable to find specific moon set time for Tucson on December 13, 2024. Websites like timeanddate.com have this data but search results did not return specific values
Unable to find specific moon set time for Tucson on December 13, 2024. Websites like timeanddate.com have this data but search results did not return specific values

Web search results: Jupiter at opposition December 7, 2024. By December 13, Jupiter rises ~1 hour before sunset. With sunset at ~18:51, Jupiter would rise ~17:51. However, some sources indicate Jupiter was rising earlier in early December (~5pm/17:00), so 16:20 seems plausible for an observing window
Web search: Jupiter was in the east around sunset in early December, reaching 50° high by 9 PM. Rise time would be in evening hours before full darkness
Web search results and astronomy.com, amsmeteors.org, imo.net: Geminids nominal ZHR is 120. Historical rates cited as 120-150. Claim of 100 minimum is below historical observed rates
Web search results: Geminids ZHR historically 120-160, with Wikipedia and multiple sources citing ZHR between 120-160 meteors per hour. Maximum of 160 matches historical data

privileged_info_summary: On December 13, 2024 in Tucson, Arizona: Sunset was approximately 18:51 (NOT 17:15-17:30 as claimed). Moon illumination was 96-98% (verified within claimed 90-99% range), approximately 2 days before the full moon on December 15. Geminids meteor shower was at its peak with expected ZHR of 120-160 meteors per hour under dark skies (though actual observations were reduced to ~15/hour due to bright moonlight). Jupiter was near opposition and well-positioned for evening observation. The metadata's claimed sunset times are significantly inaccurate by approximately 1 hour 20 minutes.
